test_description: This test case evaluates the generator model's accuracy and technical depth, assuming a perfect retrieval process.
question: [Fill] In the design of an x-ray lithography mask to reduce deleterious diffraction effects, the attenuator material and thickness are selected so that the attenuator transmits approximately _____ of the incident radiation and induces a phase shift of approximately _____ radians relative to the radiation passing through the open features of the mask.

gold_context_sources.section_title: US4890309: DETAILED DESCRIPTION OF PREFERRED EMBODIMENTS
gold_context_sources.evidence: What is claimed is: 1. Lithography mask including open features and an attenuator whose material and thickness are selected to both pass a fraction of incident electromagnetic radiation and to phase shift the radiation relative to radiation passing through the open features of the mask by approximately an odd multiple of $\pi$ radians in order to reduce deleterious diffraction effects. ... 17. The lithography mask of claim 2 wherein the attenuator transmits approximately 10% of the incident radiation.

gold_context_sources.section_title: US4890309: DETAILED DESCRIPTION OF PREFERRED EMBODIMENTS
gold_context_sources.evidence: FIG. 2 shows this reduced intensity as (I/g). A choice for the value of g could be 10, a value we use in this description to illustrate the principle of this invention. ... The only change between 6b and 6c is that the attenuator introduces a relative phase shift of . . . In practice, an intensity profile such as depicted in FIG. 6c is far more desirable ... The steeper slope of the edges of features achieved by this invention is 2 significant advantage.

ground_truth_answer: The correct answer is [10%, $\pi$]. Reasoning: The US4890309 patent document explains that to reduce deleterious diffraction effects in x-ray lithography masks, the attenuator is designed to transmit a small fraction of the incident radiation, approximately 10%, and to introduce a phase shift of approximately an odd multiple of pi (π) radians, relative to radiation passing through the open mask features. This combination of partial transmission and phase shift steepens the intensity distribution at feature edges, improving linewidth control and reducing diffraction-induced blurring.
